summary: Implement operations layer for driver
description: Convert all calls to {{libmongoc}} to use operations, allowing us to run these calls on a thread pool, and ensure thread safety of those calls.

summary: Re-sync transaction spec tests for new UnknownTransactionCommitResult label tests
description: See DRIVERS-505 for details.

summary: Resync transactions spec tests
description: See DRIVERS-500 for details.

summary: Make properties of WriteModel implementations public
description:
Right now, there is no way to inspect the properties of a {{WriteModel}} once constructed (e.g. {{UpdateOneModel}}, {{ReplaceOneModel}}, etc.). This is problematic for one of our use cases as we have both local collections represented as {{MongoCollection}} which DOES support bulk writes, and remote collections managed by Stitch that DO NOT support bulk writes. We'd like to use the same API to defer writes to these collections as we do in Java by using {{WriteModel}} implementations and simply unwrapping the filter/update for the non-bulk use case, but we cannot because the properties (filter, update, options) are not exposed.

Per discussion with [~kaitlin.mahar], this ticket proposes making them public.

summary: Handle MongoSwift name conflicts
description:
In Swift, it is possible and common that two modules may define types with conflicting names. If a user imports both of them in the same file, they can disambiguate which type they mean by prefixing it with the module name. For example, if ModuleA and ModuleB both have a type {{Foo}}, the user can refer to a specific one with {{ModuleA.Foo}} or {{ModuleB.Foo}}. 

The driver defines a class called {{MongoSwift}} that handles libmongoc initialization and cleanup via two methods, {{MongoSwift.initialize}} and {{MongoSwift.cleanup}}. This class name is problematic because it makes it difficult for users to disambiguate our types as described above. {{MongoSwift.Foo}} will look for a member of the class named {{Foo}}, rather than a type in the library named {{Foo}}. There are some workarounds for this, as described [here|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37892621/how-can-i-disambiguate-a-type-and-a-module-with-the-same-name]: users can add typealiases in separate files, or use a special import syntax for the type in question.

We have a few options for dealing with this: 

1) Do nothing. Users can use the solutions in the post linked above to work around this if/when they encounter a naming conflict. IMO, not optimal because the workarounds are non-obvious, and the compiler error you get trying to do this the normal way is confusing if you don't realize we have a class called {{MongoSwift}}. 

2) Move the {{initialize}} and {{cleanup}} functions to be free-floating and get rid of the class. This won't break anyone's existing code, as these functions would still be named {{MongoSwift.initialize}} and {{MongoSwift.cleanup}} (the MongoSwift prefix would just be unnecessary in most cases.) 

3) Move he {{initialize}} and {{cleanup}} functions to be free-floating and rename them more descriptively (e.g. {{initializeMongoSwift}}), and get rid of the class. 

4) Rename the class to something else, e.g. {{MongoSwiftDriver}}.

summary: Refactor Database/Collection initializers to take in options, instead of libmongoc types
description: As pointed out by [~matt.broadstone] in this [comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/208#discussion_r269582972], instead of configuring the libmongoc type then passing it to the Database/Collection initializer, we should just pass the options straight to the object being initialized.

summary: Support cursor in listCollections and listIndexes command
description: See DRIVERS-197 for details.

summary: Make shared access to bson_ts more explicit
description:
In the driver, all {{bson_t}} s are wrapped and owned by {{DocumentStorage}} instances, which are used as the underlying storage for {{Document}} structs. 

In many places in the driver, we provide {{libmongoc}} direct access to those {{bson_t}} s: for example, when passing in options, or when providing an empty {{bson_t}} for libmongoc to fill out with the server's reply.

This is problematic because {{Document}} s are designed to be copy-on-write; before any mutations are done on a {{Document}}, the {{Document}} checks whether its underlying {{DocumentStorage}} is uniquely referenced or not. If it's not, the {{Document}} will make a copy and cede its original storage before it makes a mutation.
When we circumvent the {{Document}}'s mutating methods and allow a {{bson_t}} to be directly modified, we perform no such checks. 

If someone else had a conflicting reference to a document where we do a sneaky mutation like this, we could inadvertently modify their document.

It also breaks Swift guarantees about mutability that a {{Document}} struct declared with a {{let}} can still be mutated.

We should consider how to make more explicit the places we pass pointers to libmongoc, and ideally differentiate between when a mutable and a non-mutable pointer is being passed.

One idea for this is to provide a {{mutating}} method on Document taking a closure, similar to {{withUnsafePointer}}, that allows the caller direct write access to the underlying {{Document}}. The {{Document}} method could do the necessary testing for a unique reference to copy storage if it's required before executing the closure.

summary: Allow a ClientSession to be provided to MongoCollection.drop and MongoDatabase.drop
description:
According to the sessions spec:

> All {{MongoDatabase}} methods that talk to the server SHOULD be overloaded to take an explicit session parameter. 

 > All {{MongoCollection}} methods that talk to the server, with the exception of estimatedDocumentCount, SHOULD be overloaded to take an explicit session parameter.

Our drop methods do not allow providing a session.

Currently, both of these methods use mongoc helpers that do not accept sessions. 

We should rewrite these methods to accept {{ClientSession}} parameters and use {{mongoc_collection_write_command_with_opts}} to execute the commands.

summary: Make options properties mutable to allow changing properties on them after creation
description:
All of the properties on our options objects are declared with {{let}}, which means you can't set them after declaring the options object.

We should change them to {{var}} so you can do something like the following:
{code:java}var opts = FindOptions()
opts.batchSize = 10{code}
We should also add empty initializers for each options struct so XCode users see that show up in autocomplete.

summary: MongoClient/Database/Collection ReadPreference getters should not return Optionals
description:
{{MongoClient.readPreference}}, {{MongoDatabase.readPreference}}, and {{MongoCollection.readPreference}} are computed properties which all return a {{ReadPreference?}}, aka an optional {{ReadPreference}}.

However, none of those getters will ever actually return {{nil}} now. They always return a value.

We should update the return type of all these computed properties so that they are non-optional \{{ReadPreference}}s instead.

summary: Use constant-time hash comparison functions
description: See DRIVERS-255 for details.

summary: Do not validate the "startingFrom" field in OP_REPLY.
description: See DRIVERS-233 for details.

summary: endSessions command no longer takes `ids` parameter
description: See DRIVERS-427 for details.

summary: Validate that w and wtimeoutMS are non-negative in WriteConcern initializer
description:
The {{WriteConcern}} spec tests contain a couple of cases checking for invalid values of {{wTimeoutMS}} and {{w}}. (Specifically, it tests negative values.)

Currently we skip these in {{ReadWriteConcernTests.testDocuments}}, because the initializer relies on libmongoc to validate the options, and libmongoc does not consider those to be invalid values.

However, we could just validate ourselves that they are non-negative. 

We should update the {{WriteConcern}} initializer that looks like this: 
{code:java}
public init(journal: Bool? = nil, w: W? = nil, wtimeoutMS: Int64? = nil) throws{code}
 

to throw a {{UserError.invalidArgumentError}} when either {{w}} or {{wtimeoutMS}} is < 0.

summary: All BSONValue types should be Hashable using 4.2+ Hashable API
description:
We should make all of our custom types that conform to {{BSONValue}} (for example {{Document}}, {{RegularExpression}}, {{ObjectID}}...) conform to the [{{Hashable}} protocol|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/hashable]. 

We should then update {{AnyBSONValue}}'s {{Hashable}} implementation to simply hash the underlying {{BSONValue}}.

summary: MongoDatabase.drop and MongoCollection.drop should allow specifying a WriteConcern
description:
A write concern can be provided to the [dropDatabase command|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/dropDatabase/] and the [drop|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/drop/#dbcmd.drop](Collection) command. 

We should
 * add {{DropDatabaseOptions}} containing a {{WriteConcern}} and allow users to optionally provide those options to {{MongoDatabase.drop}}.
 * add {{DropCollectionOptions}} containing a {{WriteConcern}} and allow users to optionally provide those options to {{MongoCollection.drop}}.

summary: AnyBSONValue.init(from decoder: Decoder) does not check all possible BSONValue types
description:
In this method we try to decoding to various possible BSON types and see which one succeeds.

However we are missing Timestamp, BSONUndefined, and DBPointer. 

This is a pretty bad bug and not the first time we've run into an issue because there is no way to exhaustively switch on all BSONValue types. 

Separately from this I have opened another ticket SWIFT-509 suggesting we figure out a way to avoid these bugs in the future.

summary: ReadConcern and WriteConcern should no longer wrap mongoc types
description:
Currently {{ReadConcern}} wraps a {{mongoc_read_concern_t}} and {{WriteConcern}} wraps a {{mongoc_write_concern_t}}.

However, in most places we use {{ReadConcern}}s and {{WriteConcern}}s , we never actually use the mongoc type for anything besides storing properties. We construct {{ReadConcern}} and {{WriteConcern}} documents ourselves which we insert into options documents. 

The only places we actually need the mongoc types are when we set a \{[ReadConcern}} or {{WriteConcern}} on a {{MongoClient}}, {{MongoDatabase}}, or {{MongoCollection}}. 

We should think about no longer wrapping the mongoc types, and just adding e.g. {{withMongocReadConcern}} and {{withMongocWriteConcern}} helpers to use in those couple of places that temporarily constructs the equivalent libmongoc types. 

This would allow us to make these types structs which seems more fitting and would cut down on any performance penalty from interop.

We could also consider doing this for {{ReadPreference}}, though we actually do use the libmongoc types every time for those since they are separate arguments in the read helpers and not part of the options structs.

summary: Support maxTimeMS for createIndexes and dropIndexes command
description: See DRIVERS-374 for details.

summary: Standardize index command options
description: Currently, the options passed to index related commands are labelled {{commandOptions}}. It would be more consistent with our other commands if they were labelled simply {{options}}.

summary: Ensure that docs are updated when public API changes are made
description:
Right now, we have to just remember to regenerate docs when a public facing change is made. It is excessive to do it on every commit, since often changes are just internal.
However, running {{make documentation}} will always generate a diff even when no public changes are made, because every file is updated with a "last updated" date. 

[~may.hoque@10gen.com] suggested that we could determine when changes are needed by taking a diff between the result of {{make documentation}} on master and the result of {{make documentation}} on the PR. There should only be a difference there when changes are actually needed. 

While we might include that as a test run on Travis, we don't want it to run on PRs, as it would force every PR with docs changes to include them in the PR to pass CI. Given the number of files that get modified, this is really annoying for a PR reviewer. So ideally, this would only run on master on Travis, and a broken build would alert the committer they need to make a second "regenerate docs" commit.

We should figure out the best way to incorporate this.

Alternatively, we could only update docs when we tag a release. in that case, we should figure out how to force regenerating docs when we do that.  The downside of that is anything we forgot to document up till then would need to be taken care of at the end when we release which might be annoying; doing it the other way would force us to document things as we write them. _However_, since it would be annoying to have to deal with forgotten documentation when we release, we could try to avoid this by turning on a {{swiftlint}} rule for complaining when a declaration does not have documentation attached to it which can now be done with [{{missing_docs}}|https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/blob/master/Rules.md#missing-docs].

Building from the above idea, a finer grained approach of running an Evergreen-like (think {{Waterfall}}) task on every commit/merge to master via Travis (and not on every PR) may allow us to deal with the issue of forgotten documentation pretty soon after closing a ticket, as opposed to during release.

summary: Ensure MongoClient, MongoCollection and MongoDatabase are thread safe
description: Make sure that these classes are fully thread safe, ensuring any internal state is appropriately locked for multithreaded use. In particular inherited properties such as {{writeConcern}}, {{readPreference}}, etc.

summary: Add APM assertions to change stream spec tests
Modify Change Stream Output Examples
description: See DRIVERS-537 for details

summary: Move listCollections filter to top level
description: In SWIFT-174, we implemented listDatabases with the {{filter}} not in the options but at the top-level. We should update listCollections to do this also.

summary: Make ServerVersion CustomStringConvertible
MongoCursor.next should check for an error before trying to get the next document
description:
Currently we just go ahead and try to call {{mongoc_cursor_next}} even if {{mongoc_cursor_error_document}} has already returned an error. 
 This might lead to overwriting some original error with an error about trying to iterate a failed cursor.

Instead, we should save off any errors returned by {{mongoc_cursor_error_document}}, and then anytime {{next}} is called check if that error is non-nil before trying to iterate again.

summary: Make MongoDatabase and MongoCollection structs
description:
For a reason we have not been able to determine, switching MongoDatabase and MongoCollection to be structs causes a Swift compiler crash in the ClientSessionTests.

I spent some time trying to create a minimal example to repro the crash but did not have any luck. We should maybe timebox spending a bit more time on that.

Otherwise I think we have to just play around with the structure of the tests until they don't cause a crash anymore.

summary: Return Connection to pool as soon as MongoCursor is exhausted
description:
Currently, if a {{MongoCursor}} uses its own {{Connection}} (i.e. not one from a {{ClientSession}}) the {{MongoCursor}} will return the {{Connection}} to the pool in deinit.

However, this could lead to situations where a user has a number of exhausted cursors lying around and exhausts the connection pool.

One change that will be needed to support this is storing the result of {{mongoc_cursor_error_document}}. Otherwise a user will not be able to check for an error after the cursor is exhausted (which they are supposed to do once {{next()}} returns {{nil}}, as {{next()}} can't throw.)

summary: Make BulkWriteOperation consistent with other Operation types
description:
While {{BulkWriteOperation}} does conform to the {{Operation}} protocol, it is very different than the other {{Operation}} types. 
It has various methods to add write models to it, and doesn't actually use the {{Connection}} passed into its {{execute}} method.

We should figure out how to refactor it such that it follows the pattern of other {{Operation}} s. All of the write models should be passed into the operation's initializer, and the execute method should actually use the provided connection.

summary: Figure out a better way to handle encoding IndexModel + IndexOptions
description: Since the options need to be at the same level in the document as the index name and keys, we have to manually encode the model and options separately and then merge them. We should figure out a cleaner way to do this.

summary: Implement index enumeration spec
description:
We should implement the [index enumeration spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/enumerate-indexes.rst] using the same approach we took when implementing the db enumeration spec (SWIFT-174).

Note that this spec is in the draft phase for some reason, though it is quite old and appears to have stabilized enough for us to implement.

summary: Implement collection enumeration spec
description:
We should implement the [collection enumeration spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/enumerate-collections.rst] using the same approach we took when implementing the db enumeration spec (SWIFT-174).

Note that this spec is in the draft phase for some reason, though it is quite old and appears to have stabilized enough for us to implement.

summary: Use Operations when creating and iterating a change stream
description:
{{MongoCursor.next}} uses {{NextOperation}} for executing most of the cursor iteration logic. Since {{ChangeStream.next}} behaves similarly and performs I/O, it should also have its logic in an operation such as {{ChangeStreamNextOperation}}. Another possible option would be to just modify the existing {{NextOperation}} to also process a {{ChangeStream}}.
{{NextOperation}} is a little weird now in that we don't execute it via executor, as its execute method ignores the passed-in connection, and we don't want to wastefully check out and back in a connection we don't use every time a cursor is iterated.

We should also Operation-ize the creation of {{ChangeStream}} s via some {{watch}} operation.

summary: Add special executor logic for operations that hold connections
description:
{{NextOperation}} and {{ChangeStreamNextOperation}} s (added via SWIFT-523) are unique in that they don't need new connections to be executed as their corresponding cursors and change streams hold onto connections.

{{WatchOperation}} is also unique in that it needs to check out a connection and then give it to the change stream. 

We should add special logic to the executor / operation protocol to handle these cases.

summary: wtimeoutMS is truncated if set to a value > Int32.max
description: The {{wtimeoutMS}} field of a write concern is spec-defined to be an {{int64}} (which we accept), but libmongoc only accepts {{int32}} s, so we truncate if necessary in order to pass to libmongoc.

summary: Decimal128.writeToCurrentPosition should pass a non-mutable pointer to bson_iter_overwrite_decimal128
description: As of CDRIVER-3069 this method takes in a const pointer. Once we bump to mongoc 1.15 as our min version we can stop passing in a mutable pointer here.

summary: Consider improvement to typing for bulk write models
description:
The InsertOneModel and ReplaceOneModel structs for {{MongoCollection.bulkWrite()}} depend on the generic CollectionType. For consistency, all bulk write models are declared within the MongoCollection class. This requires either verbose syntax (e.g. {{MongoCollection<Document>.InsertOneModel}}) or type aliases like the following:

{noformat}
private typealias DeleteOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.DeleteOneModel
private typealias DeleteManyModel = MongoCollection<Document>.DeleteManyModel
private typealias InsertOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.InsertOneModel
private typealias ReplaceOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.ReplaceOneModel
private typealias UpdateOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.UpdateOneModel
private typealias UpdateManyModel = MongoCollection<Document>.UpdateManyModel
{noformat}

We should investigate ways that this can be improved, and whether it is worth doing so.

summary: Rationalize how we handle client options provided in URI vs. options struct
description:
The options struct wins if you set a value in both places.

However, it's plausible that a user would set a ReadConcern in the URI and then think that explicitly providing ClientOptions(readConcern: nil) would disable the URI ReadConcern. It does not.

[~patrick.freed] suggests we consider an API similar to the Go driver where the user has to combine the URI and options structs themselves.

summary: Rationalize handling of server default vs. not-provided read concern, write concern
Standardize on parameter ordering for options initializers
description:
In some cases we ordered them alphabetically, in others they are just in the order the fields are declared in the struct (which is itself arbitrary), etc. 
We should follow some consistent pattern so users know what order to expect. 
We should do some research into how other libraries do this kind of thing.

summary: Add a helper method for reading in spec files
Array should conditionally conform to BSONValue
Retryable Writes tests failing assertion on latest
description: The retryable writes tests are currently [failing|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_swift_driver_tests_all__os_fully_featured~macos_1014_swift_version~5.0_ssl~nossl_test_latest_replica_set_6d4348a7164c34b40de920c625cae4851d9e6670_19_11_19_20_40_24/0?type=T#L2265] against latest due to an error being parsed incorrectly. This may be due to some error label changes in the server.

summary: Move Document.get<T: BSONValue>(_ key: String) into Tests module
description:
since we only use this in tests and it's internal, we should just move it there.

its existence may become superfluous once SWIFT-338 is completed, as it is mostly used now for pulling fields out of documents.

summary: Change streams tests failing on latest with sharded clusters
description: Most of our change streams tests are failing on sharded cluster + latest. At least one of these is caused by new behavior discussed in SERVER-44112. It is unclear whether the other failures are triggered by the first test failing (all of these tests use command monitoring and check expected events, so it is possible that the first test due to some extra or missing event would lead to all future tests failing with an off-by-one type error) or if there is a separate and unrelated issue at play. We should investigate.

summary: Reimplement errors as structs
description:
Edit: See design in WRITING-4592

The discussions in the rust driver error API reviews made me realize that our error cases are extremely rigid. For example, if we wanted to add a new field to a specific case (e.g. "errorLabels" if they weren't already), it would be a breaking change since all the pattern matching users are doing would need to be updated. In general, additive changes aren't breaking, and the specs operate under this assumption (as evidenced by errorLabels). 

To solve this, we could move the fields on the enum into a struct embedded in each of the cases (e.g. {{.writeError(WriteFailure)}}) or make the errors themselves be defined as structs. Originally, I defined all the errors as enums since I think that is generally the default way errors are defined, but it is still acceptable to define them as either structs or classes if they're more complicated. We can avoid classes though since we have no intention of doing any inheritance or have need for them to be reference types.

We should consider re-defining the errors in such a way that we can add new fields in the future without major version upgrades and whether doing so would have an adverse effect on the API.

some relevant reading:
 - [http://fernandocastor.github.io/general/2016/04/29/the-many-flavors-of-swift-error-handling.html]
 - [https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/error]

summary: build libmongoc as part of the swift driver
description: This is the majority of the work needed to fully vendor {{libmongoc}} into the Swift driver. It involves an update to {{vendor-libmongoc.sh}} to merge in {{libmongoc}} files, rename them, and build them as part of the driver build step.

summary: Correct GitHub language statistics for vendored code
description: Vendoring libmongoc has had the unintended side-effect of classifying the project as a C project in GitHub. We need to use a [gitattributes file|https://github.com/github/linguist#using-gitattributes] to inform linguist to ignore vendored code when generating code statistics.
